channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: India Doubles Gold Duty to 18.45%; Prices Jump, Demand Outlook Mixed
item.description: India's government has nearly doubled import duties on gold and silver to 18.45%, aiming to conserve foreign exchange reserves during geopolitical uncertainty. Industry leaders anticipate a temporary 10-15% dip in jewelry sales but expect strong demand driven by gold's cultural significance and investment appeal. The policy also seeks to boost domestic gold recycling.

item.title: India MSMEs Hit by ₹8.1 Lakh Crore Payment Delays; TReDS Offers Hope
item.description: India's MSMEs are crippled by ₹8.1 lakh crore in delayed payments, creating a persistent working capital crisis. Uneven credit access worsens the problem. New solutions like TReDS invoice discounting and digital credit tools are being introduced to boost cash flow and prompt payments.

item.title: Man Infraconstruction Profit Plunges 34%, Revenue Halved Amid Sector Weakness
item.description: Man Infraconstruction Ltd. reported a sharp 34.4% year-on-year drop in fourth-quarter net profit, plummeting to ₹50.5 crore. Revenue from operations was halved, falling 50.5% to ₹145.5 crore. Both the EPC and real estate segments experienced significant revenue declines. Despite the downturn, the company declared an interim dividend for FY27.

item.title: India's $500B AI Future Faces Major Adoption Hurdles
item.description: A new IBM and IndiaAI study projects Artificial Intelligence could contribute over $500 billion to India's economy by 2030, positioning it as a leading AI-driven nation. However, a significant "inflection gap" persists, with 72% of Indian organizations admitting they lag global peers in AI adoption. Key barriers include inaccessible cloud infrastructure, data quality issues, and a substantial skills shortage, with only 30% of the workforce possessing required AI literacy.

item.title: India's EV Boom Sparks $13.7 Billion Surge in Battery Imports
item.description: India's push for electric vehicles (EVs) will sharply increase its annual import bill by an estimated $13.7 billion for batteries and materials, a cost that outpaces near-term oil savings. This shift highlights new economic and geopolitical risks, especially due to reliance on China for critical minerals and battery components, despite domestic manufacturing efforts like the PLI scheme. Building self-sufficiency requires faster local production and overcoming supply chain challenges.

item.title: Gold Duty Hike Squeezes India Jewellery Volumes by 15%
item.description: India's recent gold and silver import duty hike, pushing the total levy to 18.45%, is expected to reduce jewellery volumes by 10-15% in the short term. Industry players highlight the government's aim to conserve foreign exchange reserves, though they anticipate resilient consumer demand driven by the yellow metal's deep cultural and investment significance.

item.title: Tata Motors CV Surges on Record Profit, Advances Iveco Deal
item.description: Tata Motors Commercial Vehicles achieved record operating performance in FY26 and a robust Q4, with profit after tax up 70% to ₹2,406 crore in the March quarter. Revenue increased 22% to ₹24,452 crore. Full-year revenue grew 11% to ₹77,399 crore. Despite FY26 net profit dip from exceptional items, operating profit excluding these was record-high, driven by margin expansion to 13.9% and 13.2% for Q4 and FY26 respectively. Free cash flow surged to ₹9,186 crore. The company is advancing its Iveco acquisition, expected by Q2 FY27, and secured a 70,000-unit export order for Indonesia.

item.title: Tata Motors CV Division Hits Record Profit, Offers Rs 4 Dividend
item.description: Tata Motors' Commercial Vehicles division reported a 70% year-on-year surge in net profit for Q4 FY25-26, reaching ₹2,406 crore. Revenue rose 22% to ₹24,452 crore, boosted by strong market demand and infrastructure spending. The company also declared a final dividend of ₹4 per share.

item.title: Bharti Airtel Profit Jumps 10.5%, ARPU Dip Sparks Worry
item.description: Bharti Airtel announced a sequential net profit increase of 10.5% to Rs 7,325 crore for the fourth quarter of FY26. Revenue from operations also climbed 2.6% to Rs 55,383 crore. However, the Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) saw a slight decline to Rs 257 from Rs 259 sequentially, despite a 5% year-on-year rise from Rs 245 in Q4 FY25. The company maintained a stable EBITDA margin of 56.9%. A final dividend of Rs 24 per share was recommended.

item.title: India Stocks Rise, Rupee Hits Record Low; Metals Gain, IT Dips
item.description: Indian benchmarks Sensex and Nifty closed higher, ending a four-day losing streak. Gains were led by metal, oil & gas, and consumer stocks. The Indian rupee, however, fell to a new record low against the dollar, weighed down by geopolitical concerns and high crude oil prices. Market sectors were split, with IT and auto stocks declining while metals surged. Analysts remain cautious, noting that persistent US inflation and Middle East tensions could limit India's economic growth and the Reserve Bank of India's policy options.

item.title: EPFO Automates PF Withdrawals Amid Labor Code Shift
item.description: The Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) is advancing its digital strategy by automating final provident fund withdrawal claims and inter-employer account migrations. This initiative targets a significant reduction in settlement times, potentially from over a week to under five days, enhancing operational efficiency for its 70 million members. The move is intrinsically linked to the government's implementation of new labor codes, aiming to simplify processes for employers and improve worker mobility.

item.title: Gaurav Kapoor Ventures Lands Exclusive Mandate for Mumbai Tower
item.description: Gaurav Kapoor Ventures is now the sole sales manager for The Ruby, a prime Grade-A office tower in central Mumbai. This exclusive deal replaces traditional multi-agency methods with a unified sales strategy. The Ruby tower is LEED-certified, over 1 million sq. ft., 185 meters tall, with sea views and good connectivity. This move highlights a growing trend where integrated firms manage large real estate projects with a focused approach.

item.title: Russia Assures India of Steady Energy Supply Amid Global Challenges
item.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov pledged unwavering energy supply to India, assuring New Delhi that agreements will be honored despite global challenges. The commitment covers cooperation on the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant and continued hydrocarbon shipments. Lavrov highlighted the strong friendship between the nations, noting outside efforts to strain ties.

item.title: ONGC Surges on Govt. Royalty Cut; Analysts Divided on Outlook
item.description: Oil And Natural Gas Corporation (ONGC) shares have surged recently, defying a cautious brokerage outlook citing stagnant production and projected profit declines. The company's valuation, assessed at a P/E ratio around 9.8-10.0, is lower than peers like Reliance Industries. However, a significant government royalty cut for deepwater exploration offers a considerable boost, leading to divided analyst opinions. While some maintain 'Buy' ratings with aggressive targets, others flag risks, including a recent downgrade to 'Hold'.

item.title: India AI Infrastructure: Sky-High Valuations Meet Supply Chain Hurdles
item.description: India is becoming a key global hub for AI infrastructure, drawing huge investments. Companies providing computing, fiber, cooling, and power solutions see strong demand. However, rapid growth is pushing valuations high. Execution challenges and issues like power grid limits and supply chain bottlenecks add risk, despite positive growth stories. Conflicting analyst views make it complex for investors in this high-growth, high-risk sector.

item.title: India Mandates End to 'Dark Patterns' in Digital Insurance
item.description: India's insurance sector is under a major regulatory crackdown to end 'dark patterns' – deceptive digital design tactics. The IRDAI has ordered insurers to self-assess and report compliance within 15 days, with action plans due in a month for any issues. This move aims to boost consumer trust and low penetration rates, pushing for transparent digital growth despite operational challenges.

item.title: India Approves ₹37,500 Crore Plan to Boost Coal Gasification for Energy Security
item.description: New Delhi has greenlit a ₹37,500 crore incentive scheme to promote coal gasification, targeting 75 million tonnes of coal conversion into syngas. This ambitious move aims to bolster India's energy security, slash import dependence on commodities like LNG and methanol, and stimulate ₹2.5-3 lakh crore in downstream investments. The policy leverages India's vast domestic coal reserves, seeking to diversify energy sources as geopolitical tensions escalate globally.

item.title: India Approves Rs 20,667 Cr Rail Corridor: Growth Hopes Clash with Reality
item.description: India has approved a Rs 20,667 crore, 134 km semi-high-speed rail corridor linking Ahmedabad and Dholera, set for completion by 2030-31. Part of the PM Gati Shakti Master Plan, the project aims to speed up travel, boost industry, and connect half a million residents. While praised for its vision and green benefits, investors will watch how it's financed and built, considering potential challenges and the success of local technology.

item.title: HPCL Sees Record Profit Surge on Roaring Refining Margins
item.description: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation (HPCL) achieved record annual profits in FY26, with net profit soaring 133% year-on-year to ₹17,175 crore. This performance was primarily fueled by a dramatic increase in Gross Refining Margins (GRM) to $8.79 per barrel. The company also announced a final dividend of ₹19.25 per share, totaling ₹24.25 for the fiscal year. The stock reacted positively to the news, surging significantly on May 13, 2026.

item.title: Air India Slashes Summer Routes as Fuel Costs Surge
item.description: Air India is sharply cutting international flights for its Northern Summer 2026 season, grounding services and reducing frequencies on routes to North America, Europe, Asia, and Australia. This move, hitting during its busiest revenue period, is a direct response to surging global oil prices and higher Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) costs. The airline's international capacity fell 22% in April 2026, adding to its financial pressure after losses exceeded ₹22,000 crore in the last fiscal year.

item.title: India Backs ₹37,500 Crore Coal Gasification Plan to Cut Fuel Imports
item.description: India has greenlit a ₹37,500 crore plan to boost coal gasification, aiming to convert 75 million tonnes of coal into syngas by 2030. The goal is to sharply reduce the country's ₹2.77 lakh crore fuel and chemical import bill. The strategy aims to mobilize up to ₹3 lakh crore in investment and create 50,000 jobs, leveraging India's large coal reserves despite a global push for cleaner energy.

item.title: Air India Cuts International Flights Amid Soaring Jet Fuel, Airspace Issues
item.description: Air India is significantly reducing international flights and suspending six routes from June through August. This move is driven by high jet fuel prices and airspace restrictions making some long-haul routes unprofitable, despite the airline operating over 1,200 international flights monthly. The decision highlights financial strain and a broader aviation industry challenge.

item.title: PNC Infratech Wins ₹766 Cr UP Projects, Faces Bid and Partner Risks
item.description: PNC Infratech Ltd. wins two major EPC projects in Uttar Pradesh worth over ₹766 crore. A joint venture secured a ₹571.81 crore Ganga River bridge project, while PNC also won a ₹194.40 crore Lucknow flyover project. These wins highlight ongoing infrastructure growth in UP, but its reliance on L1 bids and its partner's financial health need watching.

item.title: Bajel Projects Lands ₹300 Cr Order Amid High Valuation Debate
item.description: Bajel Projects announced securing a significant EPC order for a 400kV transmission line, valued between ₹200-₹300 crore. The contract, awarded by a Power Grid Corporation of India SPV, aligns with India's renewable energy evacuation goals and has a 21-month execution period. This latest win adds to the company's order book, yet the stock trades at a P/E multiple substantially higher than its sector peers, raising valuation concerns.

item.title: Bitcoin Hits $82.5K Hurdle Amid Economic Uncertainty
item.description: Bitcoin is facing strong resistance between $82,000 and $82,500, a crucial test after a downturn in early 2026. While many holders remain profitable, breaking this barrier is key to confirming a new uptrend. The crypto market is influenced by sticky inflation, cautious central bank policies, and limited liquidity. Competitors like Ethereum show higher volatility, while Solana focuses on technical upgrades.

item.title: LPG Subsidy Misses Rural India as Fuel Costs Drive Return to Firewood
item.description: Many rural Indian families, especially in Chhattisgarh and Madhya Pradesh, still use firewood for cooking despite government LPG subsidies like the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY). High LPG refill prices and rising transport costs are pushing households back to traditional fuels. This shows a persistent affordability gap, where access to clean fuel doesn't always translate to practical use, affecting health and environmental targets.

item.title: KRT Distributes ₹716 Cr as Net Operating Income Jumps 14%
item.description: Knowledge Realty Trust (KRT) announced a ₹716.6 crore distribution for unitholders in the quarter ending March FY26. The payout was driven by a strong 14% surge in net operating income, reaching ₹1,053.3 crore. KRT also reported significant leasing activity, securing 1.1 million square feet in Q4 FY26 and maintaining 92% portfolio occupancy. Management is optimistic about the year ahead.

item.title: India's Pension Tax Maze Fuels Demand for Advisors
item.description: India's pension tax rules are complex and varied, posing a significant challenge for retirees. This complexity drives demand for expert financial and tax advisory services, helping individuals avoid penalties and optimize their retirement income.

item.title: India Raises MSP for Oilseeds, Cotton to Counter Supply Chain Woes
item.description: India's government has boosted Minimum Support Prices (MSP) for 14 kharif crops for the 2026-27 season. Sunflower seed and cotton saw the biggest jumps. This move aims to ensure fair prices for farmers and promote crop diversity. It also comes as global fertilizer supplies face severe disruption due to geopolitical tensions, signaling India's effort to boost domestic output and cut import reliance amid rising costs.

item.title: BYD India to Raise EV Prices July 1 Due to Rupee Slide
item.description: BYD India is increasing prices on its electric passenger vehicles by 1-2% from July 1, 2026. The company cites ongoing currency fluctuations and rising supply chain costs as reasons. The move comes as BYD aims to hold its premium position amid tough competition and currency challenges.
